rulings.Case Title: METROPOLITAN DIRECT PROPERTY & CASUALTY VS. MATTHIAS STELICKI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Service
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 23, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT MATTHIAS STELICKI's Motion To Quash Service Defendant Matthias Sielicki's motion to quash service and to vacate judgment is granted. The purported service of the summons and complaint on Mr. Sielicki on November 7, 2010 is quashed and the default and default judgment entered against Mr. Sielicki on February 21, 2012 and May 11, 2012 are set aside. The presumption of proper service has been rebutted by Mr. Sielicki's evidence showing that: a) he did not live and had never lived at the address where the purported service and the purported diligent efforts were made to personally serve him and b) the amended proof of service contained information at substantial variance with the initial proof of service strongly suggesting that the amended proof of service was not in fact accurate. Most significantly, plaintiff Metropolitan Direct Property and Casualty Insurance Company has provided no evidence that the address where the purported service and the purported diligent efforts were made was a place where Mr. Sielicki might be found or where he lived or worked. The default and default judgment are vacated per CCP 473 (d). (Ellard v. Conway (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 540, 544 ("a default judgment entered against a defendant who was not served with a summons in the manner prescribed by statute is void.").) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Sielicki is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE CO. VS. JAVIER DIAZ MASIAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant Interline Brands, Inc. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Lisa Fulton; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 23, 2018, Line 15, Cross Defendant Interline Brands, Inc. Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Lisa Fulton; Request For Sanctions Cross-defendant Interline Brands, Inc.'s motion to compel the deposition of Lisa Fulton is granted and its request for monetary sanctions is denied. Ms. Fulton's deposition must occur on or before October 26, 2018. The primary ground asserted to oppose this motion is that all information known by Ms. Fulton relating to this case is covered by the work product privilege of plaintiff State Farm General Insurance Company. However, State Farm has not submitted any evidence showing that Ms. Fulton was retained by or on behalf of any attorney, so State Farm has failed to establish the foundation for work product. While it may be that Ms. Fulton has little or nothing pertinent to say about State Farm's subrogation claim, that is not a basis to preclude her deposition, especially given the allegations of the cross-complaints. In all events, instead of advising Ms. Fulton not to attend her deposition which is what appears that State Farm did, the proper course was for State Farm to file a motion for protective order. Because there appears to be substantial justification for State Farm's positions, no monetary sanctions are awarded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Interline is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOE VS. RAMLA CO., LTD., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Court Order To Allow Pretrial Discovery Of Defendant'S Financial Condition In Support Of A Punitive Damage Claim; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 23, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE's Motion For A Court Order To Allow Pretrial Discovery Of Defendant'S Financial Condition In Support Of A Punitive Damage Claim. Pro Tem Judge Roger Mead, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff seeks an order authorizing pre-trial discovery of defendant's financial condition in support of a punitive damages claim. Plaintiff has not made a showing sufficient to establish "that there is a substantial probability that the plaintiff will prevail on the claim pursuant to Section 3294" and therefore is not entitled to such discovery. However, a defendant may be required to identify documents and witnesses so that the plaintiff can issue trial subpoenas to ensure the availability of evidence of the defendant's profits and financial condition in the event the trier of fact finds that a defendant is guilty of malice, oppression, or fraud in accordance with Section 3294. Civil Code Section 3295(c). Accordingly, plaintiff's motion is granted to the following limited extent: Not less than 10 days before trial commences, Defendant Ramla Co. Ltd. shall identify documents in its possession that are relevant and admissible for the purpose of establishing its profits or financial condition referred to in Civil Code Section 3295(a), and the witnesses employed by or related to Defendant Ramla Co. Ltd. who would be most competent to testify to those facts. The documents and witnesses shall be identified in sufficient detail for the issuance of a trial subpoena to compel the production of such documents and witnesses at trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rmead@folgerlevin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ASAD ALLAH ET AL VS. THE POTRERO HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 23, 2018, Line 7, CROSS DEFENDANT LAMBERT/O'CONNOR DEVELOPMENT 450RI LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Cross-complainant Lambert/O'connor Development 450RI LLC's motion for summary adjudication on the fourth and fifth causes of action in Lambert's first amended cross-complaint seeking an order that cross-defendant R.I. and 17th LLC has a duty to defend Lambert against the claims alleged by cross-complainant Whole Foods Market California, Inc. is denied in its entirety. Whether there is a contractual duty to defend and what circumstances a duty to defend is triggered is determined based on the language of the parties' agreement. The rules of interpretation in Civil Code 2778 are "default rules" and only apply if the parties' agreement does not provide for contrary rules. The Lambert/RI agreement provides that the "Indemnitor shall be entitled to defend." This language is not materially different from the contract language that was construed in Gribaldo, Jacobs, Jones & Associates v. Agrippina Versicherunges A. (1970) 3 Cal.3d 434, 448 to provide for a right but not a duty of the indemnitor to defend. Gribaldo remains good law and was discussed with approval by Crawford v. Weather Shield Manufacturing, Inc. (2008) 44 Cal. 4th 541, 556-57. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: LANCE JENSEN VS. CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 23, 2018, Line 8, PLAINTIFF LANCE JENSEN's Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint Plaintiff Lance Jensen's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted, the trial date is continued to December 9, 2019, all discovery deadlines are triggered off the continued trial date, and Mr. Jensen is required to file a motion for class certification no later than March 4, 2019 to be heard no later than April 15, 2019. Mr. Jensen has leave to file his first amended complaint no later than October 31, 2018. It will not be deemed filed. The court exercises its discretion to grant this motion based on California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings at any stage of a case and the elimination of any legal prejudice to defendant California Physicians' Service by the continuance of the trial date. In an effort to avoid any further trial continuances, the court is setting deadlines for the filing and hearing of a class certification motion. rulings.Case Title: CHAO HONG TAN VS. JUSTIN CHEUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) PES-18-302013 WITH CASE CGC-17-560310
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 23, 2018, Line 10, PLAINTIFF CHAO HONG TAN MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) PES-18-302013 WITH CASE CGC-17-560310 Plaintiff Chao Hong Tan's motion to consolidate this case with case PES-18-30213 is granted. The two cases are consolidated for all purposes. The lead case is PES-18-30213 and all further filings will be in that case only. All future dates set in this case are vacated. Consolidating the two cases is in the interests of judicial economy and will avoid possible inconsistent results. Making the probate case the lead case is in recognition that both cases raise issues that are properly handled in the probate division of this court, not the civil division. While Ms. Tan could have acted with greater diligence, her failure to do so is not a basis to deny this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KAREN Y UCHIYAMA VS. GLORIA LOPEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Preclude Plaintiff From Giving Evidence Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 454; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of James Martinez In Support; [Proposed] Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 23, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT GLORIA LOPEZ's Motion To Preclude Plaintiff From Giving Evidence Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 454. Pro Tem Judge Roger Mead, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Gloria Lopez's unopposed motion is granted. CCP Section 454 provides: "It is not necessary for a party to set forth in a pleading the items of an account therein alleged, but he must deliver to the adverse party, within ten days after a demand thereof in writing, a copy of the account, or be precluded from giving evidence thereof." On August 16, 2018, Defendant served a demand for a bill of particulars pursuant to CCP Section 454; Plaintiff has failed to provide an account setting forth the items of the account alleged in the complaint. Accordingly, pursuant to CCP Section 454 Plaintiff is precluded from giving evidence of the items of the account alleged in the complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rmead@folgerlevin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LEERINK REVELATION HEALTHCARE FUND I, L.P ET AL VS. BALAJI K SRINIVASAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Balaji K. Srinivasans Notice Of Hearing On General Demurrers And Demurrers To The First And Fourth Causes Of Action In Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 23, 2018, Line 13, DEFENDANT BALAJI SRINIVASAN's Hearing On General Demurrers And Demurrers To The First And Fourth Causes Of Action In Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint The motion will be heard by Judge Fleming at 9:00 a.m. Defendant Balaji Srinivasan's demurrer to the first and fourth causes of action for specific performance and breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing in plaintiffs Leerink Revelation Healthcare Fund I, L.P and Leerink Revelation Healthcare Fund II, L.P.'s second amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiffs have indicated that they are no longer pursuing a cause of action for specific performance and it is dismissed. Under Delaware law the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, which inheres in every contract and proscribes a party's arbitrary or unreasonable conduct that prevents the other party from receiving the benefits of the bargain, "comes into play. . . only where a contract is silent as to the issue in dispute." Narrowstep, Inc. v. Onstream Media Corp. (Del. Ch., Dec. 22, 2010, No. CIV.A. 5114-VCP) 2010 WL 5422405, at *10. Here, the essence of plaintiff's claim is that Mr. Srinivasan used the ROFR process in bad faith to prevent the condition precedent of the contract from occurring. Such conduct is proscribed by the language of section 3.1 requiring parties to use reasonable best efforts "to cause such conditions to be satisfied at the earliest reasonably possible date." Just like in Narrowstep, Inc. v. Onstream Media Corp, defendant's repeated bad faith manufacturing of delays in the closing of the merger was expressly proscribed by the provision requiring defendant's "reasonable best efforts to effect an expeditious closing of the merger." Id at 11. Furthermore, section 3.1 requires the reasonable best efforts of the parties "to satisfy their respective obligations with respect to the conditions set forth [in the contract]" and the non-exercise of the right to first refusal within a prescribed time is a condition listed in section 4.1. Plaintiff's allegations that Mr. Srinivasan breached the implied covenant by not complying provisions of the ROFR agreements is covered by and is duplicative of plaintiffs' breach of contract cause of action. Because plaintiffs have not shown how amending the pleading would cure its deficiencies, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Srinivasan is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
